entry.title: Re: New person in SAP
entry.content:
Hello,

I can relate to your situation because my path into SAP was very similar.

I did not start my career as an SAP consultant. I spent more than 15 years working in logistics and supply chain operations, where I became an SAP key user working with MM, warehouse processes, inventory, goods movements, and shipping.

My advice is: do not underestimate your key user experience. Many successful SAP consultants started by understanding the business first, and then they learned the system in more depth.

The fact that you have access to SAP and an SAP PRESS MM book is actually a great advantage. My recommendation is not to simply read the book like a novel. Use a practical approach:

Read → Practice → Understand the business process

For example, if you read about a Purchase Order, go into the SAP system and explore:

  • What is the purpose of this transaction?
  • What master data is involved?
  • What happens before and after this step?
  • Which departments are involved?

This way, you are not just learning clicks and transactions; you are learning the business process.

In my own journey, I combined several learning methods:

  • Reading SAP books (especially MM and Business Partner)
  • Taking structured online courses
  • Practicing directly in the SAP system
  • Learning integration between modules and understanding end-to-end processes

Today, I work as an SAP SD Support Consultant in an international company using SAP S/4HANA. My daily work involves investigating incidents, supporting users, understanding business processes, and working with integrations between SAP, Salesforce, internal applications, and other systems.

One important lesson I learned is that SAP is about system thinking. A change in one area can affect many other areas, so understanding the complete process is more valuable than memorizing transactions.

For your case, I actually think MM is a very good place to start, especially because you already have some exposure to it. Focus first on the fundamentals:

  • Material Master
  • Procurement process (PR → PO → Goods Receipt → Invoice Verification)
  • Inventory management
  • Basic integration with finance and logistics

Do not worry too much about becoming a consultant immediately. A good path can be:
Key User → SAP Support / Junior Analyst → Junior Functional Consultant → Consultant

Companies highly value people who understand real business operations because SAP exists to support those processes.

My final advice would be: be consistent. Study a little every day, take notes, create your own process diagrams, and whenever possible, test what you learn in the system.

The combination of business knowledge + SAP knowledge + good communication skills is what will help you grow in this field.

Good luck with your SAP journey!

entry.title: DCD: “Recheck” releases sales order although no SD document changes were made; PDF snapshot issue
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

we are currently working with SAP S/4HANA Financial Supply Chain Management, specifically Credit Management / Case Management and the Documented Credit Decision process.

We are facing two issues and would appreciate your input.

1. “Recheck” releases the sales order although no changes were made

A sales order is blocked by credit management and a Documented Credit Decision case is created.

Within the documented credit decision, the processor has the following options:

  • Recheck
  • Release
  • Reject

If we choose Recheck, the system releases the sales order, although no changes were made to the SD document after the credit block was created.

From our understanding, a recheck should validate the current credit situation again. However, we would like to understand why the sales order is released by using Recheck, even though the document itself has not changed.

Our questions are:

  • What exactly is evaluated when using Recheck in a Documented Credit Decision?
  • Is it expected standard behavior that the sales order can be released after a recheck, even if the SD document has not been changed?
  • Which data changes outside the SD document can influence the result of the recheck, for example:
    • credit exposure,
    • credit limit,
    • payment behavior,
    • risk class,
    • credit segment data,
    • open items,
    • collateral or other credit-relevant data?
    • Is there a way to analyze why the recheck results in a release?
    • Are there specific logs, customizing settings, BAdIs, or SAP Notes related to this behavior?

2. PDF snapshots cannot be generated

In addition, we are trying to generate PDF snapshots for the Documented Credit Decision.

The required forms have been created and assigned. We also executed the Credit Decision Setup Check, and it does not show any errors.

However, the PDF snapshot generation still does not work as expected.

Our questions are:

  • Which prerequisites are required for PDF snapshot generation in Documented Credit Decisions?
  • Are there specific form assignments, output settings, or technical services that need to be active?
  • Which logs or transactions can be used to analyze why the PDF snapshot is not generated?

Are there known SAP Notes or limitations for PDF snapshots in S/4HANA Credit Management / Case Management?

Any hints, configuration checks, or SAP Notes would be highly appreciated.

We also used "UKM_DCD_SETUP_CHECK".

Best regards,
Jan-Hendrik

entry.title: Request for Workaround on Intercompany Returns Credit Memo Issue
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support Team,

For Intercompany Returns, we have created the Returns Delivery (LR) and the customer Credit Memo (G2). However, when attempting to generate the Internal Credit Memo (IG), the system reports that no copy control exists between LR and IG.

As per SAP Note 3360228, IG Credit Memos should be created only through the LR2 delivery type. Unfortunately, we have already processed approximately 300 Intercompany Returns using the returns order type CBRE instead of CBAR, as recommended.

Reversing all 300 documents would be highly impractical. We kindly request your guidance on the best possible workaround. Specifically, could manual Credit Memos be created directly in FI, or would it be feasible to configure copy control between LR and IG?

Your support on this matter would be greatly appreciated.

Best regards,

entry.title: LAM (Linear Asset Management) - remove markers from with references to "where used list"
entry.content:
Hello,

we send LAM data from our GIS system to SAP. I maintain the GIS and the LAM data in the GIS — I am not very familiar with SAP.

Situation:
When a LAM changes, markers are sometimes removed. These markers are not automatically removed from the LAM in SAP; they are only marked (with the prefix DEL). I have to manually edit the LAM in SAP using transaction IK82 and remove these markers from the LAM.

Screenshot above - marker should be removed but is only labeled with prefix "DEL" - I need to remove it manually in SAP....

Problem:
If a marker (or the SAP object the marker refers to) has a where used list, I must first edit that where used list — the reference to the marker must be removed there. Only then can I remove the marker from the LAM. However, a marker can have 50 of such usage records — processing all usage records manually is an extremely time-consuming task.

Screenshot above - I cant just delete a marker if the marker has references to "where used list",,,,,

Question:
Are there ways in SAP EAM to remove these markers from a LAM easily? Without having to edit every usage record?

Many thanks

entry.title: Re: My Leave Request On behalf of
entry.content:
The issue is most likely related to missing HCM authorizations for the personnel number selected in the "On Behalf Of" scenario.

Although the BAdI implementation correctly enables the functionality and returns the employee in the selection list, the manager/user must also have authorization to read the required HR master data of the selected employee.

Please verify the backend HCM authorizations (PFCG roles and authorization objects like p_orgin) for the relevant infotypes, for example:

  • IT0001 (Organizational Assignment)
  • IT2001 (Absences)
  • IT2006 (Absence Quotas)

The error "No authorization to display data of employee number" usually indicates that the user does not have sufficient authorization to access the selected employee's HR data in the backend system.

entry.title: Re: no code/ low code report
entry.content:
Dear @abdelrahman_khaimse5f2c1,

In Public Cloud both requirements need to be designed using the released Public Cloud extensibility options

For the API requirement, the first option is to use the Custom Fields app. A custom field can be added to the relevant business context and then enabled for the relevant released OData API, provided that the API and business context support custom-field extensibility. After publishing the field the API metadata should be checked again to confirm that the field is available

If the required standard API or business context does not support the custom field the standard API should not be modified directly. In that case, the clean-core approach is to build a custom API using Developer Extensibility / RAP in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud ABAP Environment, based only on released SAP objects

For the ABAP report requirement, this should not be approached as a classic SE38-style ABAP report. In Public Cloud the right solution depends on the business purpose:

  • for analytical reporting, use Custom Analytical Queries and Services
  • for an interactive custom report, build a RAP / SAP Fiori elements app
  • for scheduled or background processing, use an Application Job Catalog Entry and Application Job Template
  • for external consumption, expose a custom RAP/OData API

More details below:

  • SAP Help – Creating Custom Fields
  • SAP Help – Editing Custom Fields
  • SAP Help – Developer Extensibility
  • SAP Help – Custom Analytical Queries and Services
  • SAP Help – Creating a Job Catalog Entry and a Job Template
  • SAP Developers – Build an SAP Fiori App Using RAP
  • SAP Developers – Creating a Custom Inbound API in SAP S/4HANA Cloud ABAP Environment
  • SAP Community – ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model RAP

Summary:

In Public Cloud, custom fields can be added to a standard API only where the released API and business context support extensibility. For the ABAP report, the recommended Public Cloud approach is not a classic on-premise ABAP report but a Custom Analytical Query, RAP/Fiori app, Application Job or custom RAP/OData API, depending on the requirement

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: How to handle a schema change in SAP Document AI
entry.content:
Hi

We have build a process on SAP standard schema SAP_purchaseOrder_schema and we have made templates for different customers and to each template is attached a few documents.

Now a new requirement to extract a special danish number called an EAN nummer. In this context an EAN number is a "digital customer address" for use in EDI when we send EDI invoices. Usually we use the Tax ID to identify the customer, but some customers does not include the tax ID in their purchase order PDF. Instead they include the EAN number which can also be used to identify the customer.
TO include the EAN field i need to make a modified customer specific copy of the standard schema.

That was the background and now to the question. When I make a new schema, can I then reassign the templates including the attached documents to this new schema or do I have to build new templates?

As the existing templates does not know the new field, I guess I will have to start all over and create new templates and that lead me to next question. Will this schema change influence on existing training? (If existing training exist - I don't understand how this training works in combination with templates - no, I don't understand how it works at all).

Best Regards
Thomas

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 18 - RFC for UUIDs
Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 18 - RFC for UUIDs
Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 18 - RFC for UUIDs
SAP EPPM Financial Planning: Introducing Custom Value Types for Flexible Cost Splits
entry.content:
Business Scenario

In SAP Project System, projects often distinguish between CAPEX and OPEX costs by using dedicated WBS elements.

For example:

  • WBS Element A → CAPEX
  • WBS Element B → OPEX

When integrating these costs into SAP EPPM Financial Planning, the business requirement is frequently to display CAPEX and OPEX costs in separate financial planning categories.

The challenge is that for both WBS elements cost will appear with the same Cost Element or Cost Center/Activity Type.

Since the standard EPPM aggregation logic only considers these dimensions, both costs would be aggregated into the same financial planning category.

By introducing custom value types, additional business logic can be incorporated into the financial integration process.

 

Spoiler
The approach described in this blog can also be applied to other business scenarios requiring additional financial dimensions.

 

Implementation

1. Create Custom Value Types 

The first step is to define custom value types. A value type represents the origin or classification of financial data within the integration between Controlling and Portfolio Management.

Custom value types can be maintained in the Portfolio Management Financial and Controlling Integration customizing ( Integration with Other SAP Components -> SAP Portfolio and Project Management -> Portfolio Management -> Customize Portf. and Proj. Mgmt Value Type ). In case your SAP EPPM and SAP Project System are runnin on 2 different instances, please configure the custom value types on both.

For the CAPEX/OPEX scenario, two custom value types can be introduced:

 

Value TypeDescription
ZCCAPEX
ZOOPEX 

 

These value types will later replace the standard value type during data transfer.

 

2. Maintain Integration Customizing

The next step is to maintain the integration customizing that maps the custom value types to the corresponding View–Category–Group combinations.

Separate entries must be created for each custom value type.

ViewCategory GroupValue TypeCost Element[other Attributes]
Actual CostCAPEX Internal ServicesZC8150000 
Actual CostCAPEX External ServicesZC4711000 
Actual CostOPEXInternal ServicesZO8150000 
Actual CostOPEXExternal ServicesZO4711000 

 

Spoiler
Important: Do not create additional integration entries for the original value type.

In this example, actual costs are originally transferred with value type 04 (Actual Cost). Once the custom value types are introduced, the standard value type 04 should no longer be used in the integration customizing for these scenarios.

 

3. Implementation of BAdI RPM_FICO_INT

The final step is to implement BAdI RPM_FICO_INT.

This enhancement allows the value type to be modified during the financial integration process.

For actual costs, the method:

IF_EX_RPM_FICO_INTF~CONVERT_BW_WBS_COST

can be used.

Depending on the integration scenario, other methods of the same BAdI may be more appropriate.

Example Logic:

For each entry in CT_COST_OBJECT_DATA:

  1. Read the assigned WBS element
  2. Determine whether the WBS belongs to CAPEX or OPEX.
  3. Use an identifier such as:
    • WBS numbering conventions
    • User-defined fields
    • Project attributes
    • Object class
  4. Replace value type 04 with:
    • ZC for CAPEX
    • ZO for OPEX

Example:

  • WBS PRJ-100-C→ Value Type ZC
  • WBS PRJ-100-O → Value Type ZO

After the value type has been changed, the standard integration framework processes the data according to the customizing maintained in step 2.

 

Result

After implementing the enhancement, financial data is automatically aggregated into the correct:

  • View
  • Category
  • Group

No additional enhancements or reporting logic are required.

The standard EPPM financial planning functionality can therefore be extended to support business-specific dimensions such as CAPEX/OPEX while preserving the standard aggregation mechanism.

 

Conclusion

Custom value types provide a flexible way to enrich the standard SAP EPPM financial integration process.

By combining:

  • Custom value types
  • Integration customizing
  • BAdI RPM_FICO_INT

organizations can introduce additional financial dimensions that are not covered by the standard cost element and cost center logic.

Although this article uses a CAPEX/OPEX split as an example, the same approach can be applied to other business requirements, such as:

  • Investment categories
  • Business units
  • Funding sources
  • Other customer-specific reporting dimensions

entry.title: Triggering Workflow on Goods Receipt (GRN) Cancellation in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We have a business requirement in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition to trigger a workflow whenever a Goods Receipt (GRN) is cancelled/reversed.

Current Scenario:

  • A Goods Receipt is posted against a Purchase Order.
  • Subsequently, the Goods Receipt may be cancelled (for example, through a reversal movement).
  • At the time of GR cancellation, the business wants a workflow to be triggered automatically.

Requirement:

  • Trigger a workflow when a GR cancellation/reversal is posted.
  • The workflow may be used for notifications, approvals.

Questions:

  1. Is there any standard Flexible Workflow available for Goods Receipt cancellation in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?
  2. Are there any released Business Events related to Material Document Reversal / Goods Receipt Cancellation that can be used to trigger a workflow?
  3. Can this requirement be achieved using SAP Build Process Automation, Event Mesh, Situation Handling, or any available extensibility options?
  4. Has anyone implemented a similar requirement in the Public Cloud environment?

Any guidance, best practices, or relevant documentation would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you.

entry.title: Cross ATP Check in Two-Tier ERP: Smarter Availability Commitments with Joule
entry.content:
Introduction

In today's distributed enterprise landscape, subsidiaries and headquarters operate as interconnected but distinct entities — each with its own inventory, fulfilment capabilities, and customer commitments. When it comes to archetype “sales office as subsidiary” where regional subsidiary functions as sales office and depends on headquarters for fulfilment of quantities through intercompany replenishment between the two tiers or supply to customer directly using drop-shipment from HQ then One of the most persistent challenges in a Two-Tier ERP setup is ensuring accurate, real-time Available-to-Promise (ATP) checks that span across both tiers.

In above scenario the challenge Inside sales representative deals with while booking customers’ orders and promising qty and dates to customers is stock availability information at the other tier is available for confirmation only once the stock is booked via firm sales order creation on another Tier playing role as the supplier. This gap can be addressed with Custom Joule skills capability developed on Joule Studio that enables subsidiaries to check stock availability at headquarters in real time, make reliable delivery commitments to customers, and orchestrate fulfilment seamlessly across tiers — all through natural language interactions with Joule

This blog explores how the Cross ATP Check works, the business scenarios it enables, and why it is a transformative capability for Two-Tier ERP deployments.

What Is the Two-Tier ERP Cross ATP Check?

The Cross-system ATP Check is a real-time availability check mechanism that bridges the inventory visibility gap between a Tier 2 (subsidiary – SAP Cloud ERP) and a Tier 1 (headquarters – SAP Cloud ERP Private). Rather than operating in silos, the subsidiary can now — through Joule — query the HQ's stock levels, receive availability dates, and act on that information instantly, without manual data exchange or delays.

This capability is custom Joule skill built on Joule Studio and is not delivered as part of standard product.

Key Business Benefits:

  • Seamless availability check across subsidiary and HQ
  • Faster and more reliable customer delivery commitments
  • Optimized inventory utilization across tiers
  • Boosted operational agility
  • Sales Order and Purchase Order creation directly via Joule

Three Core Business Scenarios :

Below are the three scenarios we have developed as capability show-case of Custom Joule skill on Joule Studio for Two-Tier Cross ATP check.

Scenario 1 — Delivery Date Commitment Based on HQ Stock Availability

A customer requests a product at the subsidiary. The subsidiary uses Joule to query HQ inventory, receives the date and quantity available, and communicates a reliable delivery commitment to the customer — factoring in HQ availability and shipping lead times. HQ then ships directly to the customer on the committed date.

Value:  The subsidiary commits to a date based on live HQ data, improving customer satisfaction and credibility.

Scenario 2 — Customer Fulfilment (Partial Local, Partial HQ)

A customer orders may only be partially fulfilled from local stock at subsidiary . Joule first checks local inventory. If fully available locally, the order is fulfilled directly. If only partially available, Joule checks HQ for the remainder. Two orders are created via Joule: A Sales Order for the local portion (immediate delivery) and a Third-Party Sales Order for the remainder, which automatically  creates a Purchase Order to HQ  and a subsequent sales order at HQ via standard best practice 2EJ/2EL . HQ ships the balance directly to the customer.

Value: Enables seamless split fulfilment — maximum available quantity is delivered immediately while the rest is routed to HQ automatically.

Scenario 3 — Optimized Procurement from Multiple  Locations

When the subsidiary needs to replenish its own stock, Joule evaluates inventory availability across HQ locations and other subsidiary locations (e.g., Tier 1 and Tier 2A). The subsidiary selects the best location based on availability and fulfilment capability. A Purchase Order is created and transmitted to the selected HQ via the Two-Tier ERP integration (2EJ). The chosen location fulfils and ships the order.

Value: Introduces intelligent, real-time multi-source procurement — the subsidiary makes the optimal sourcing decision without manual coordination between systems or teams.

Conclusion

The Two-Tier Cross ATP Check is infused with Joule AI capabilities through custom Joule skills, bringing cross-tier inventory intelligence directly into a single conversation. A subsidiary sales representative can check HQ availability, commit to a delivery date, create the sales order, and trigger the inter-company purchase order — all without switching systems. What once required manual coordination across two tiers now happens in one Joule interaction.

 

Stay Tuned for more details in Show and Tell session for Two-Tier ERP on 15th July 3.45pm IST. 

Note : This capability is custom Joule skill built on Joule Studio and is not delivered as part of standard product.

entry.title: From Transactions to Transformation: The Value of an Enterprise Account
entry.content:
Most suppliers on SAP Business Network today are using a standard account—and that’s a great place to start. It allows you to digitize your procurement and sales processes and begin collaborating seamlessly with your customers.

But it doesn’t have to stop there.

A recent webcast reintroduced the enterprise account, highlighting that transacting on the network is really just the beginning. As your business grows—with more transactions and more customers—SAP Business Network can evolve from a simple transactional tool into a strategic asset.  And an enterprise account helps you get there, by giving you the capabilities you need to handle growing complexity, strengthen relationships with existing customers, and increase your visibility with new ones. In short, it helps transform your presence on the network into a competitive advantage.

 

🔄System integration: from manual to seamless

An enterprise account includes everything you get with a standard account—and then goes much further, especially when it comes to handling higher volumes and working with multiple buyers.

One of the most powerful capabilities is system-to-system integration.

By connecting your internal systems directly to SAP Business Network, information flows automatically between you and your customers. Purchase orders, confirmations, and invoices can be exchanged without manual intervention—while still being validated against buyer-specific rules. And unlike traditional EDI integrations, this setup is designed to be more accessible and scalable. You benefit from:

  • A wide range of pre-built connectors
  • A compatibility dashboard to guide your setup
  • Smart cXML templates that reuse your previous configurations and apply industry standards

The result? Faster implementation, less complexity, and a true “one-to-many” integration model—combining the reach of the network with the efficiency of automation.

 

📊Actionable business data: turning insight into impact

Good decisions start with good data—and SAP Business Network delivers plenty of it.

With an enterprise account, you gain access to a wide range of insights to help you better understand and grow your business:

  • Reports: Choose from 23 report types and schedule them to run automatically. These give you a clear snapshot of your activity over time.
  • KPI & Benchmark dashboards: Track key performance indicators like invoice cycle times, responsiveness, and change order rates. These are benchmarked against industry data drawn from hundreds of millions of transactions.
  • Aggregated analytics: Get a broader view of your operations across customers and regions.
  • Demand Trends: Spot new opportunities earlier by analyzing anonymized network data representing over $7 trillion in annual commerce. This gives you a unique perspective on buying patterns and market trends.

In other words, you’re not just managing transactions—you’re gaining the insights needed to optimize performance and identify growth opportunities.

 

🤝Additional support when you need it

As your activity grows, so does the need for expert support—and that’s exactly what an enterprise account provides.

You get access to a full range of support options, including:

  • Live chat and the ability to schedule a phone call with support specialists
  • Dedicated assistance for key projects like system integration or catalog enablement, available from start to completion
  • Free consultations on topics of your choice to help you get the most out of your account

And for suppliers at the Platinum tier, there’s even more: a dedicated account manager to help you develop your strategy and expand your presence.

 

🌟Build trust and stand out to new customers

Here’s something many suppliers don’t realize: buyers often prefer working with suppliers on enterprise accounts.

When companies onboard new suppliers, they look for partners who are already active on SAP Business Network—and those with enterprise accounts tend to stand out. Why? Because they signal:

  • Faster responsiveness
  • Greater process ownership
  • Stronger support capabilities for complex operations

In a competitive environment, an enterprise account can help you build credibility, increase visibility, and position yourself as a reliable, scalable partner.

 

💡Value that goes beyond cost

While a standard account is free to use, an enterprise account comes with a cost based on your activity. But when you look at the value it delivers, the investment quickly makes sense.

According to a recent IDC research, suppliers using SAP Business Network enterprise accounts achieve an average 416% return on investment over three years. This takes into account revenue growth, better governance, improved compliance, and the ability to cross-sell and upsell more strategically. The ROI here isn't just about cutting costs — it's about what becomes possible when you have full visibility and access to the right buyers.

 

🚀Ready for what’s next?

If your activity on SAP Business Network is growing—and likely to keep growing—it’s worth planning ahead.

An enterprise account gives you the tools, insights, and support you need to scale confidently. From automation and analytics to dedicated assistance, it helps you move from simply participating in the network to truly thriving on it.

An alternative way to grow your business is to become easily visible to new customers looking for your goods and services. To increase your chances at being picked, and to validate your presence on SAP Business Network, we advise you to check out the Promote subscription. Simply log it to your account and visit the Store tab to find out more.

If you found this article interesting, more documentation on the different capabilities is available on our SAP Business Network for Suppliers page.

entry.title: Re: Extending CKM3N in S/4HANA: A Practical Approach for Multi-Material and Multi-Period Analysis
entry.content:
Hi,

Thank you for your comment and for sharing your approach. I'll definitely take some time to read your article.

In my implementation, I followed a layered CDS architecture. Rather than extending CKM3N directly, I modeled the Material Ledger data through multiple CDS layers, progressively enriching and aggregating the information before exposing it through a dedicated Consumption View.

The Consumption View itself is intentionally simple. Most of the complexity is concentrated in the underlying data model, where filtering, aggregation and performance optimization are handled before the data reaches the consumption layer.

For example, the Consumption View mainly defines the analytical contract, including filters, semantics and aggregation behavior:

@VDM.viewType: #CONSUMPTION

@Consumption.filter: {
    mandatory: true,
    selectionType: #RANGE,
    multipleSelections: true
}

@Consumption.valueHelpDefinition: [{
    entity: {
        name: 'I_MaterialVH',
        element: 'Material'
    }
}]
key Material;

@Consumption.filter: {
    mandatory: true,
    selectionType: #RANGE,
    multipleSelections: true
}
key FiscalYear;

@Consumption.filter: {
    mandatory: true,
    selectionType: #RANGE,
    multipleSelections: true
}
key FiscalPeriod;

Business measures are exposed using semantic annotations and default aggregations, for example:

@Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'CurrencyCode'
@DefaultAggregation: #SUM
PriceDifference;

@Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'CurrencyCode'
@DefaultAggregation: #SUM
ExchangeRateDifference;

@Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'CurrencyCode'
@DefaultAggregation: #SUM
ActualValue;

The implementation itself is composed of multiple CDS layers. The Consumption View is only the final layer responsible for exposing the analytical model to different consumers such as ALV, Fiori Elements, OData and Excel.

Since the focus of the article was the analytical concept rather than the implementation details, I preferred not to include the complete data model.

I'm currently preparing another article showing how the same Consumption View can be used to build a Fiori Analytical List Page using CDS UI annotations. I believe that article will complement this one quite well.

Thank you again for sharing your approach!

entry.title: Re: Payment Terms Limitation for Installment Payments Abo...
entry.content:
Hi Priscilla Dias,

Number of Days field in Payment Terms is a standard SAP field and supports a maximum value of 999 days. This limitation is part of the standard product design and cannot be extended through configuration.

For payment terms that require due dates beyond 999 days, SAP recommends using the Fixed Day and Additional Months fields. These fields allow the system to determine the due date by adding the required number of months to the baseline date and, if required, setting a fixed day within the resulting month.

Therefore, for a 60-month installment scenario where the due date extends beyond 999 days, the recommended approach is to use Additional Months (and Fixed Day, where applicable) rather than relying on the Number of Days field. There is no standard SAP functionality available to increase the 999-day limit of the Number of Days field.

Best Regards,
Jyothi

entry.title: Start Your SAP Career on Coursera: Two Certificates to Get You There
entry.content:
Looking to start a career in SAP but not sure where to begin? You’re not alone and there’s now a clear, accessible path. Nearly 200 million learners are already using Coursera to start or change their careers, and SAP is part of that journey. 

Coursera offers two SAP Professional Certificates, designed for accessibility and real career momentum. Self-paced, available in over 25 languages, needing no prior SAP experience, and completable in as little as 12 weeks. 

They are built for early-career professionals, students, and anyone looking to transition into SAP-related roles, offering a structured way to build confidence before stepping into the ecosystem.   

Each Coursera certificate targets a distinct role in the SAP ecosystem: 

  • SAP Technology Consultant Professional Certificate: Builds the core consulting skills SAP customers and partners look for, from requirements gathering and stakeholder management to solution design and navigating full SAP implementation lifecycles using SAP Activate. 
  • SAP Business Analyst Professional Certificate: Focuses on business analysis fundamentals, process modelling with SAP Signavio, data analysis with SAP Analytics Cloud, and how to bridge the gap between business requirements and technical delivery. 

Both are available on Coursera and carry ACE® (American Council on Education) credit recommendations. This means learners may be able to apply their learning toward academic credit at participating U.S. and Latin American universities. 

 The pathway to SAP Certification 

 These certificates don’t stop at foundational knowledge; they open the door to what comes next.  From there, learners can deepen their skills on SAP Learning site through self-paced courses and expert-led learning journeys. 

Ready to take the first step? Whether you’re starting your own SAP journey or supporting the next generation of talent, these certificates offer a practical and proven entry point into the ecosystem.

entry.title: Re: The Financial Statement Version is tied to a released...
entry.content:
#GenAI-Assisted Content

Hello,

Thank you very much for your question. 

I asked our internal chatbot for support, and here's the response. Please check whether the suggestions help you resolve your issue:

Resolving Transport Request Issues for Financial Statement Version Activation

When you encounter the error message ‘Task BEMK900345 is not in status modifiable’ while attempting to activate a Financial Statement Version (FSV), it indicates that the task is part of a transport request that has already been released. To resolve this issue, follow these steps:

  1. Create a New Transport Request: Ensure that you have created a new transport request, which you have already done.

  2. Assign the New Transport Request: Unfortunately, the system does not automatically allow you to select a new transport request when editing the FSV. You need to manually assign the new transport request to the changes you are making.

  3. Use the Correct Application: Use the app ‘Manage Financial Statement Versions’  to make changes to the FSV. After saving the changes, the system should prompt you to activate the FSV, allowing you to select the new transport request.

  4. Check Transport Request Status: Ensure that the new transport request (BEMK900346) and task (BEMK900347) are in a modifiable status. If they are not, you may need to create another transport request or check with your system administrator.

  5. Activate the FSV: Once the new transport request is correctly assigned, attempt to activate the FSV again.

For more details, see the following information:

Knowledge Base Article: 3399551 - Financial Statement Version Hierarchy not Transport to P system - SAP S/4HANA Cloud

Financial Statement Version

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: Re: SAP BPA automation: get token and pass to an API
entry.content:
Hi @shruti_chhabra ,

Ideally adding an await befor the api call should have worked.
Also, for fetching the token, it should be a GETcall and not a POST. 

const response = await irpa_core.request.call(options1); 

If you want to use the response later in automation, define an output parameter in your custom script and return the data in the variable as below.

    return {
        apiResponse: response
    }

Generally, 401 error is for unauthorized access. Seems your user does not have proper authorization for making the call or password is not correct.

Thanks,
Vaibhav

entry.title: Re: Request for Updated Crystal Reports Runtime (.NET 64-...
entry.content:
Often those CVE's can be reported but CR Products do not use the internal API that is affected.

I've passed it on to R&D to look into.

In the mean time you can add an exception, R&D won't update the version just because if it doesn't affect CR products.

PS - All of BOE/CRS/CR products use the same runtime so it's not simple to replace one file. Requires QA'ing all of them.

Also note CR for VS does not release patches, full builds only, next release won't be until end of year or January 2027.

In the mean time you may have to go back to a previous version before the reported issue.

entry.title: Re: Unable to assign new GL account to custom movement ty...
entry.content:
#GenAI-Assisted Content

Hello,

Thank you very much for your question. 

I asked our internal chatbot for support, and here's the response. Please check whether the suggestions help you resolve your issue:

Solution for Custom Movement Type Account Determination

To address the issue of not seeing the general modification ‘ZBR’ in the dropdown for automatic account determination, you can consider the following options:

  1. Create a Custom Access Sequence:

    • You can create a custom access sequence by copying the standard access sequence  and adding your custom general modification ‘ZBR’. This allows you to define specific account determination rules for your custom movement type. For more information, see Change Account Determination Access Sequence - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition,

  2. Enhancement Options:

    • If creating a custom access sequence does not resolve the issue, you may need to explore enhancement options. This could involve custom development to modify the account determination logic to recognize the ‘ZBR’ modifier.

If none of these options work for you, please let us know.

Kind regards,

Henrike
